Identity.Name: Pset_StingSpatialCodes
Identity.Definition: Canonical short codes attached to IFC spatial entities (IfcBuilding, IfcBuildingStorey, IfcZone). The codes here are the SOURCE OF TRUTH for STING's LOC / LVL / ZONE tag tokens — the matching Pset_StingTags fields on each element MUST reference a code defined on a spatial entity in the same project. This makes the tag taxonomy reference the native IFC spatial structure rather than duplicate it.

Governance.StandardsBasis: ISO 19650-2 §5.3 spatial structure conventions; STING tag grammar v5.0

Property.name: LocationCode
Property.Definition: Short building code (e.g. BLD1, MAIN, NORTH). Attached to IfcBuilding (and optionally IfcSite). Tag token LOC must equal this LocationCode on the IfcBuilding the element is contained in.

Property.name: LevelCode
Property.Definition: Short level / storey code (e.g. GF, L02, B1, RF). Attached to IfcBuildingStorey. Tag token LVL must equal this LevelCode on the IfcBuildingStorey containing the element via IfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure.

Property.name: ZoneCode
Property.Definition: Short zone code (e.g. Z01, WARD-A, OR-SUITE). Attached to IfcZone. Tag token ZONE must equal this ZoneCode on an IfcZone the element is grouped into via IfcRelAssignsToGroup.

Property.name: ZoneCategory
Property.Definition: Semantic category of the zone (Fire / Acoustic / Smoke / Clinical / Pressure / HVAC / Security). Disambiguates zones when an element belongs to multiple — e.g. one IfcZone for fire compartmentation + one for acoustic isolation.
Property.Notes: The StingZoneCategoryCodes enumeration is not in the Tier 1-3 enum bundle; it's a Tier 6 (composition/structure) deliverable. For now the property accepts any IfcLabel and IDS rules validate downstream.

Property.name: SortOrder
Property.Definition: Integer ordering hint used by browser / schedule sort. Smaller = appears first. Two storeys at the same elevation can use SortOrder to disambiguate (e.g. mezzanine above GF gets SortOrder above GF + below L01).

Property.name: HumanName
Property.Definition: Human-readable name of the spatial container, e.g. "Main Hospital Building" / "Second Floor — Surgical Suite" / "Acute Ward A". Distinct from the IfcBuilding.Name attribute (which may be the official building number) and from LocationCode (the short tag code). Used in schedules, mobile UI, and printed sheets.

Rule.Description: An element's Pset_StingTags.Location must equal the LocationCode of the IfcBuilding the element is contained in (or its IfcSite if no building applies).
Rule.FailMessage: Element LOC does not match a building's LocationCode in the project.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Open STING Tags panel, set Location to a code defined on an IfcBuilding's Pset_StingSpatialCodes.

Rule.Description: An element's Pset_StingTags.Level must equal the LevelCode of the IfcBuildingStorey the element is contained in via IfcRelContainedInSpatialStructure.
Rule.FailMessage: Element LVL does not match the storey's LevelCode.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Verify the element is on the correct storey, then resync LVL token via STING.

Rule.Description: An element's Pset_StingTags.Zone must equal the ZoneCode of at least one IfcZone the element is assigned to via IfcRelAssignsToGroup.
Rule.FailMessage: Element ZONE does not match any assigned IfcZone's ZoneCode.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Assign the element to the correct IfcZone, then set the Zone token to match.

Rule.Description: Every LocationCode in the project must be unique across IfcBuildings. Two buildings cannot share a code.
Rule.FailMessage: Duplicate LocationCode detected on multiple IfcBuildings.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Reassign one of the colliding buildings to a unique LocationCode.

Rule.Description: Every LevelCode in the project must be unique within an IfcBuilding. Two storeys in the same building cannot share a code.
Rule.FailMessage: Duplicate LevelCode detected within a single IfcBuilding.
Rule.RemediationInstruction: Use SortOrder to disambiguate or rename one of the colliding storeys.
